root_motivation: Special days deserve a stamp rather than a haiku or a glyph fallback — birthdays, anniversaries, the day a PRD shipped, the day the printer arrived. PRD-daily-receipt's classifier already returns `special` when summary.special_stamp_id is set; that field is the integration point. This PRD writes the producer for that field: the stamp catalog format (date-specific + recurring JSON files), a manage-stamps CLI (day-stamp add|list|render|which|seed), and the lookup convention that day-summarize and day-haiku already cite. Without this crate the third day-type in the daily-receipt vision (workday/quiet/special) is unreachable.
user_persona: Joe Yen on wintermute. Wants stamps that fire on special dates so the daily strip carries texture: 'printer-arrives' on 2026-05-27, 'New Year' recurring on 01-01, birthdays added with `day-stamp add`. Catalog lives at $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/daily-receipt/stamps/ with ~/.claude/ fallback. Lookup is deterministic: date-specific (YYYY-MM-DD.json) shadows recurring (MM-DD.json). Producer for daily-receipt's special path; consumer side is daily-receipt-printer's today flow which shells out to `day-stamp render today --out <path>` when summary.special_stamp_id is set.

acceptance_criteria.description: `day-stamp seed` against an empty $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/daily-receipt/stamps/ writes 4 starter stamp files. Re-running `seed` does not overwrite; exits 0.
`day-stamp which 2026-05-27` prints `printer-arrives` to stdout. `which 2026-05-28` (no stamp) prints nothing and exits 0.
`day-stamp which 2026-01-01` matches the recurring `01-01.json` stamp. Verified for at least two distinct years (2026, 2030) in the same test.
Date-specific stamp file shadows the recurring file for the same MM-DD: when both `2026-12-31.json` and `12-31.json` exist, `which 2026-12-31` returns the date-specific one.

`day-stamp add --id foo --title T --date 2026-05-30 --line "Hello"` creates `2026-05-30.json` with the expected shape; running again with the same `--id` and date errors with exit 4 (no silent overwrite).

`day-stamp render printer-arrives --out /tmp/p.escpos` writes a non-empty ESC/POS byte stream that begins with ESC '@' (0x1B 0x40), contains the stamp title bytes verbatim, ends with feed-and-cut bytes (0x1D 0x56 0x42 0x00). Daily-receipt AC2 compatibility.

`day-stamp render` with no `lines` and `glyph_seed: 42` renders a 24x24 raster glyph (reuses daily-receipt's renderer via dep, or shells out to `daily-receipt render` with a fake summary — pick the simpler path).
`day-stamp list --json` prints a JSON array of all stamps with full metadata; sortable by `created_at`.

Each stamp file's `lines` violating the schema (>=13 lines, or line >40 graphemes) is rejected at `add`-time with exit 5; `render`-time of an existing-but-malformed file also exits 5 with the offending line numbered in stderr.

scope: Binary `day-stamp` with subcommands: which [date], list [--kind K] [--json], add --id --title --date --line ... [--kind] [--category] [--size-hint] [--glyph-seed], render <id|today> --out <path>, seed

Source layout: src/main.rs (clap dispatch), src/lib.rs (re-exports), src/catalog.rs (file IO + date/recurring lookup + shadow rule), src/render.rs (ESC/POS bytes assembly: ESC@ init, centered title/subtitle/lines, feed lines per size_hint, GS V B 0 cut), src/validate.rs (lines count 1..=12, grapheme width <=40, kind enum)

Stamp JSON schema: { id, kind: birthday|anniversary|milestone|named-day|custom, title, subtitle, lines: [..], glyph_seed: Option<u64>, size_hint: small|medium|large, category, created_by, created_at }
Date-specific file <YYYY-MM-DD>.json shadows recurring <MM-DD>.json; both are checked; date-specific wins; `which` returns the id of the winning stamp or empty on no match
Seed catalog (4 files; idempotent, never overwrite): 01-01.json New Year (recurring), 12-31.json Year ends (recurring), 2026-05-27.json Printer arrives, 2026-05-22.json Daily-receipt PRD drafted
Atomic writes (tempfile + rename) for add and seed; same filesystem rename for atomicity
Exit codes — 0:success/no-match-with-empty-output, 2:bad CLI args, 4:add collision (id+date duplicate), 5:schema violation
Deps strictly: clap, chrono, serde, serde_json, toml. NO network. NO unsafe. Optional dep on `unicode-segmentation` for grapheme width — count as 1 of max_deps
LOC budget: <=350 src, <=300 tests per PRD §Crate shape
non_goals: Automatic milestone detection (build skill writing stamps/<today>.json on ship) — separate follow-on PRD
Notifications when a stamp fires — discovered by reading the strip
Stamp drawing tools — stamps are hand-edited JSON in v0.1

consumed_by: day-haiku (skips composition when special_stamp_id is set)
daily-receipt-printer (today flow calls day-stamp render when special_stamp_id is set)

five_whys_trace.a: PRD-daily-receipt §3 named 'Stamp (special day)' as the third day-type but did not specify storage format or selection rules. daily-receipt/src/classifier.rs already returns `special` when summary.special_stamp_id is set; that field is the integration point. Without this crate the third day-type is unreachable and the strip's texture stays at workday/quiet only.

five_whys_trace.q: Why a separate crate rather than extending daily-receipt?
five_whys_trace.a: daily-receipt is pure-byte-stream and testable without devices. Stamps are content (catalog + renderer) — different lifecycle. Day-summarize and day-haiku need to query 'is today special?' without pulling the byte-encoding harness. Separate crate keeps both pure: daily-receipt produces bytes from a summary, day-stamps produces bytes from a stamp id.

five_whys_trace.q: Why JSON files keyed by date rather than a SQLite ledger or TOML manifest?
five_whys_trace.a: One stamp per file makes hand-editing in $EDITOR trivial, makes `cp` / `git mv` / version control work without parsing logic, and matches the existing daily-receipt convention of ~/.claude/daily-receipt/<date>.json for daily payloads. Lookup is just a stat(2) on two paths (date-specific then recurring).

five_whys_trace.q: Why both date-specific (YYYY-MM-DD) and recurring (MM-DD) with date-specific shadowing?
five_whys_trace.a: Recurring captures the structural rhythm (New Year, anniversaries). Date-specific captures the one-shot milestone (the day the printer arrived). The shadow rule means a recurring 12-31 stamp can be overridden for one specific year — e.g., a year-end-letter PRD that ships its own 2026-12-31.json with the longer strip without disturbing the base recurring entry.

five_whys_trace.q: Why atomic writes (tempfile + rename) for add when the catalog isn't multi-writer?
five_whys_trace.a: /build runs every 5 minutes and the user may run `day-stamp add` interactively; if a tick's write races with an interactive read, partial JSON would crash the consumer. rename(2) is atomic on the same filesystem; tempfile-in-same-dir + rename is the cheapest correctness story. Mirror of daily-receipt-printer's AC4 atomicity model.

ambiguities_resolved.question: How is the catalog directory created on first use?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: `day-stamp seed` runs fs::create_dir_all on the resolved catalog path (XDG first, fallback ~/.claude). `which`/`list` create the dir only if no stamp files exist and return empty results — no error on first-use empty catalog.

ambiguities_resolved.question: What does `day-stamp render today` do when no stamp matches today?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Exits 0 with no output. The printer wrapper checks `which today` first (which is the canonical 'is today special?' query) and only calls `render today` when that is non-empty. Defensive: `render <id>` for an explicit id that doesn't exist exits with code 5 (schema/lookup violation).

ambiguities_resolved.question: AC7 glyph-only render — does day-stamps depend on daily-receipt as a path dep, or shell out?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Shell out to `daily-receipt render --glyph-seed N --quiet --out <path>` when stamp.lines is empty and stamp.glyph_seed is Some. Path resolution: $DAILY_RECEIPT_BIN > `which daily-receipt`. Mirrors daily-receipt-printer's resolution. Falls back to a stub 24x24 raster (all-on pixels) if daily-receipt is unavailable so AC7 is verifiable in isolation.

ambiguities_resolved.question: AC9 schema-violation at render time — what bytes go to stderr?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Format: `day-stamp: invalid stamp <path>: line <N>: <reason>` where N is 1-indexed and reason is one of `>40 graphemes (got G)` or `too many lines (got L, max 12)`. Exit 5. No partial bytes written to --out.
